signals.evidence: Praveen Seemundun proposed the next meeting himself: 'Does Monday 11.30 am BST work for you?' (SoW / Merlin review thread).
SoW sent hours after the review call: 'You now have all the contractual documents: MSA, OF1, and SoW.'

CPO Florence Tinsley-Roy attended the review with Stephen Cook, Paras Sood and Praveen Seemundun ('Thank you very much for the constructive call today'); she had personally rescheduled it on 6 Jul ('Can we please move to 11am Tuesday or Wednesday?').
'Florence CPO hired one of our senior consultant who will be PM for her he will join end of August' (Next Step 8 Jul): the economic buyer committed implementation resources before signature.
'She wants to sign before end of July (She is in vacation in 2 weeks)'; 'Finalizaion of contracts => 24th of July'; buyer-proposed SoW/Merlin review Monday 13 Jul.
Stephen Cook and Paras Sood, silent through four reminders since 12 Jun ('0 Feedback'), joined the constructive 8 Jul review.
Publicis OneTrust compliance self-assessment completed ('The assessment is now completed.', Brice Phosarajdavong to Praveen); Ariba supplier creation running since 22 Jun; NDA executed 29 Jun.
Emeline Le Foyer returned the completed functional questionnaire: 'Please find attached the document completed on the functional part... might be adjusted pending on new operating model.'
CloseDate pushed 2026-07-15 to 2026-07-30 (second push in ten days; 13 to 15 Jul on 29 Jun), aligned to Florence's stated end-of-July signature window.
Praveen Seemundun (Category Manager IT Services, Publicis Re:Sources) replaces the vacationing Emeline Le Foyer as commercial counterpart for the final mile.

'she did not came back on Merlin for I Contract to be put as option instead of core module which would have a significant impact (50K if we consider discount) on TCV (let's see if Florence will insist or not)'.
MEDDPICC 2.0 records 'No competition'; no rival named in any field, email or next-step entry from 11 May through 8 Jul 2026: sole-source.

top_rationale: Sole-source Commit deal moving at buyer pace: CPO Florence Tinsley-Roy selected Zycus off her prior PwC deployment and is driving signature before her late-July vacation.

8 Jul was the pivot day: constructive review with Florence, Stephen Cook, Paras Sood and Praveen Seemundun; the SoW went out the same day, completing the contract pack (MSA, OF1, SoW) with finalization targeted 24 Jul.

Buyer skin in the game: Florence hired a senior Zycus consultant as her project PM (joins end of August); Publicis executed the NDA (29 Jun), completed OneTrust compliance (2 Jul) and is running Ariba supplier creation (since 22 Jun).
Live risks: the close date was pushed twice in ten days (13 to 15 to 30 Jul) and the 50K Merlin for iContract core-vs-option question has sat unresolved since 29 Jun.
Continuity watch: Emeline Le Foyer, who drove four OF1 rounds, is on vacation; Praveen Seemundun now carries the commercial thread into the buyer-proposed 13 Jul review.

Coverage note: no Avoma recording exists for the current cycle; meeting content is reconstructed from the dense two-way email trail and the deduped Next Step journal, so coverage is flagged partial_low_evidence.

drivers: ✅ Sole-source selection with the CPO driving: Florence Tinsley-Roy picked Zycus off her PwC experience and wants to sign before end of July (Next Step 8 Jul); no competitor named in any source since 11 May.
✅ Contract pack fully delivered: MSA legal clauses resolved 29 Jun, final OF1 sent 30 Jun, SoW sent 8 Jul; buyer-proposed review 13 Jul and finalization targeted 24 Jul.

✅ Buyer commitment beyond words: Florence hired a senior Zycus consultant as her project PM (joins end of August) before signature; Publicis executed the NDA (29 Jun), completed OneTrust compliance (2 Jul) and is onboarding Zycus in Ariba (22 Jun).
✅ Process validators re-engaged: Stephen Cook and Paras Sood, silent through four reminders since 12 Jun, joined the constructive 8 Jul review, closing the last decision-criteria gate.

⚠️ Close date pushed twice in ten days (13 to 15 Jul, then 15 to 30 Jul) with Florence's vacation from around 22 Jul leaving zero slack behind the 24 Jul finalization. ► Lock signatories and the e-sign route with Praveen at the 13 Jul review so signature can execute in her absence.

⚠️ Merlin for iContract core-vs-option is unresolved and worth about 50K of the 250K TCV; Emeline never came back on it (29 Jun). ► Win it on value at the 13 Jul Merlin review and get Florence's written core-scope confirmation by 15 Jul.
win.focus_now: Land the 13 Jul SoW review and Merlin demo, then convert it immediately into signature logistics before Florence leaves around 22 Jul.
win.ceiling_applied: Stage anchor Vendor Selected (72) + rubric +25 (preference, champion, exec access, competitive all maxed; business case partial) + engagement pulse +13 + CRM trend +1, then the Vendor Selected ceiling of 85 was crossed under rule 5.1's explicit allowance (ForecastCategory = Commit AND stage >= Vendor Selected); forecast-conviction ceiling (80) not binding on a Commit deal.
win.exception_statement: Ceiling crossed above 85 per rule 5.1 (Commit + Vendor Selected). Additionally, hard paper evidence shows the deal operating beyond its recorded stage: BAFO 24 Jun, MSA with all legal clauses resolved 29 Jun, final OF1 30 Jun, SoW delivered 8 Jul, finalization targeted 24 Jul; scored against contract-finalization reality. ► Advance the stage to Negotiation / Contract In Progress in Salesforce to reflect reality.

drivers: ✅ Buyer-side forward actions inside 14 days: Florence rescheduled and attended the 8 Jul review, and Praveen Seemundun proposed the 13 Jul follow-up himself (pull, not push); buyer-confirmed next milestone satisfies the strict-reading bar for Accelerating.

✅ Deliverables landing in both directions: Publicis legal's revised contract 26 Jun, Emeline's completed functional questionnaire 1 Jul, OneTrust assessment completed 2 Jul, Zycus SoW delivered 8 Jul.
✅ Two-way email cadence nearly every business day from 26 Jun to 8 Jul across four buyer contacts; no one-way chasing, no passivity (buyer initiates scheduling and compliance gates).
✅ Next Step fresh at 90% confidence with buyer-anchored dates: buyer-proposed review 13 Jul, contract finalization 24 Jul, signature before end of July set by Florence herself.

⚠️ Close date pushed twice in ten days (13 to 15 to 30 Jul), a -4 drag even though it tracks Florence's own signature window. ► Hold the 24 Jul finalization by locking signature logistics at the 13 Jul review.

⚠️ Emeline Le Foyer, the commercial driver of four OF1 rounds, is on vacation mid-close and Praveen is new to the thread. ► Open the 13 Jul call with a two-minute recap of agreed commercial history to keep Praveen aligned.
mom.focus_now: Convert the buyer-proposed 13 Jul SoW/Merlin review into confirmed sign-off and signature logistics before Florence's vacation starts around 22 Jul.

sum.headline: Publicis held a constructive review with CPO Florence Tinsley-Roy's full team and Zycus delivered the SoW the same day, completing the contract pack (MSA, OF1, SoW), though the close date moved out to 30 Jul.
supporting: Publicis proposed the next step itself: Praveen Seemundun asked for a SoW and Merlin for iContract review on Monday 13 Jul.
Florence has hired a senior Zycus consultant as her project manager (joining end of August) and wants to sign before her late-July vacation, with contract finalization targeted 24 Jul.
sum.as_of_note: As of 8 Jul 2026, the last business day; no qualifying activity yet on 9 Jul.
